items.title: The outback jobs disappearing as fuel costs hit tourism

children.content: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

gallery.altText: A young woman bottle feeds a calf and a lamb.

thumbnail.altText: A young woman bottle feeds a calf and a lamb.

avatar.alt: A young woman with brown hair smiles.

items.title: 'Fierce and defiant' disability advocate remembered after drowning

children.content: Ellen Fraser-Barbour is being remembered for her tireless advocacy and love of nature after her death at the age of 35.

image.altText: A disability advocate with her friend.

items.title: Man says he was acting in 'self-defence' when he kicked a dog twice in the head

children.content: A northern suburbs man says he had been bitten and was acting in self-defence when he kicked a dog that was tethered to the tray of a ute in a Bunnings car park.

image.altText: Maya was allegedly kicked to the head while in a Bunnings car park

items.title: Butters found guilty of abusing umpire as Port claims 'glaring hole in the evidence'

children.content: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters is found guilty of umpire abuse at the AFL Tribunal, despite strongly denying allegations he asked umpire Nick Foot "how much are they paying you?".

articles.title: Butters asked 'why did you pay that?' Koch says

children.content: Port Adelaide chairman David Koch says the AFL club will back Zak Butters "to the hilt" amid dispute over what was said to an umpire during Sunday night's loss against St Kilda.

image.altText: AFL player Zak Butters at a media conference.

articles.title: How the AFL's gamble with conflict of interest concerns didn't pay off

children.content: The AFL gambled that concerns over a conflict of interest with one of its umpires wouldn't be an issue, but that call has come back to bite them — and it's not the umpire's fault.

image.altText: An umpire smiles while being interviewed during a press conference.

items.title: Blow to cartography in Australia as The Map Shop prepares to close

children.content: One of Australia's few remaining cartography shops is about to close in Adelaide, prompting questions about where people can buy the critical documents, particularly those travelling in remote areas where mobile coverage is limited.

image.altText: Man stands in front of shelf of maps

items.title: Billionaire's 'slow moving car crash' leads to individuals being investigated by ASIC

children.content: After years of financial red flags, corporate watchdog ASIC confirms it has multiple investigations underway into Sanjeev Gupta's troubled empire, following multiple collapses that have left hundreds of workers facing an uncertain future.

image.altText: GFG Alliance executive chair Sanjeev Gupta.

items.title: Residents near cement plant raise concerns about more plastic being burnt for fuel

children.content: Residents living near a large cement plant in Adelaide are concerned about the owner wanting to burn more plastic as fuel to replace natural gas.

image.altText: aerial image of a manufacturing plant with river to the right and cloudy sky in background

items.title: Qantas cancels flights between Adelaide and Mount Gambier amid fuel shortages

children.content: Qantas will no longer fly to South Australia's largest regional city, citing fuel shortages and declining demand for flights to Mount Gambier.

image.altText: Qantas Mount Gambier

items.title: Rate rises, spending cuts and cost blowouts: Councils squeezed by fuel crisis

children.content: Some South Australian councils may hike rates above inflation this year as they grapple with cost blowouts, spending cuts and project delays caused by the fuel crisis.

image.altText: two diggers in a fenced off area with flattened dirt next to a road with cars passing by

items.title: One Nation approaches independent MPs as it makes the case to be SA's opposition

children.content: One Nation SA leader Cory Bernardi has approached independent MPs to make the case for his party — instead of the Liberals — to be recognised as the state's official opposition.

image.altText: composite of Matt Schultz, Lou Nicholson and Geoff Brock

items.title: Reptile remains believed to be a crocodile found dumped on side of road

children.content: Reptile remains — which are believed to be from a crocodile — have been collected by authorities after they were found dumped on the side of an Adelaide road.

image.altText: A crocodile in brown water looking straight on at the camera close-up like a portrait.

items.title: PM in Brunei for fuel talks as Chalmers heads to Washington

children.content: Anthony Albanese will leverage Australia's food exports while in Brunei in a bid to shore up long-term fuel supplies. Meanwhile, Treasurer Jim Chalmers is off to Washington following the release of the IMF's latest global economic outlook. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese presser

items.title: IMF releases sobering economic outlook as strait remains disrupted

children.content: The International Monetary Fund paints three economic outlook scenarios that each get progressively worse depending on how long Middle East energy supplies are disrupted.

image.altText: A man walks along a rock wall with ocean and ships in the background.

items.title: What a 'super El Niño' would mean for Australia's weather in 2026

children.content: Australia's weather is expected to shift rapidly thanks to the probable emergence of an incoming El Niño, meaning drought, extreme heat and bushfires are on the way.

articles.title: Flood watches issued as former Cyclone Maila brings days of heavy rain

children.content: Minor flood watches have been issued for several Far North Queensland catchments as former Tropical Cyclone Maila delivers sustained heavy rainfall, with forecasters warning transport routes could be disrupted.

image.altText: Man walking dog and drinking coffee on a beach

gallery.altText: A sunset reflecting on a lake with boats sailing and a table with seating in the foreground silhouetted.

thumbnail.altText: A sunset reflecting on a lake with boats sailing and a table with seating in the foreground silhouetted.

avatar.alt: ABC meteorologist Tom Saunders smiling in profile photo

items.title: Trump lashes out at European ally Meloni amid tensions over pope and NATO

children.content: The US president says Giorgia Meloni "does not care" about Iran getting a nuclear weapon, after the Italian PM defended the pope and cancelled a military deal with Israel.

image.altText: Giorgia Meloni and Donald Trump shaking hands.

items.title: Hidden cameras in change rooms cause regional town hall to postpone events

children.content: An investigation into hidden cameras in change rooms has forced the Horsham Town Hall to postpone events in western Victoria.

image.altText: Photo on the corner of the street, looking down two sides of the town hall building with a has a sign: "Horsham Town Hall"

items.title: Overcrowded boat sinks in rough seas with 250 people on board

children.content: The UN says the boat sank with about 250 people on board, including children, in the Andaman Sea "due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding".

image.altText: BANGLADESH COAST GUARD

items.title: Leaving your laptop on for the commute? Here's the impact

children.content: While modern laptops are good at saving battery life and keeping cool, extended periods in sleep mode could still be putting a strain on them over time.

gallery.altText: A woman standing behind a desk wearing a loose button-up shirt reaches to shut down the lid of a laptop

thumbnail.altText: A woman standing behind a desk wearing a loose button-up shirt reaches to shut down the lid of a laptop

avatar.alt: A headshot of a woman with long light brown hair and blue eyes

items.title: How The Babadook inspired the 'scariest movie you'll ever hear'

children.content: Filmed in director Ian Tuason's childhood home and based on his own experiences caring for his dying parents, Undertone has become a sleeper hit.

image.altText: A woman in a dimly lit room screams

items.title: Quiz: We're going on a trip — don't forget your toothbrush

children.content: What are the pyramids made out of? And which Caribbean island doesn't appear in the Beach Boys song Kokomo?

image.altText: Hong Kong street at sunset time

items.title: Prince Harry and Meghan kick off Australia visit with stop at Melbourne hospital

children.content: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex have begun their multi-day visit to Australia in Melbourne, meeting with patients at a children's hospital.

articles.title: As the Sussexes return to Australia, 'unusual' commercial engagements are raising eyebrows

children.content: Harry and Meghan will be travelling under their titles of Duke and Duchess of Sussex, and will appear at two major, ticketed events as well as making the rounds of various charities and causes.

image.altText: prince harry and his wife Meghan leaving a cathedral in the UK smiling and happy.

gallery.altText: Meghan Markle talking to a young girl
Prince Harry greets a boy as he visits the Royal Children's Hospital in Melbourne.

thumbnail.altText: Meghan Markle talking to a young girl
Prince Harry greets a boy as he visits the Royal Children's Hospital in Melbourne.

items.title: How AI has transformed the way some students are learning

children.content: Thousands of students are using AI to transform the way they learn, but many remain concerned about what the technology means for the environment, jobs and creativity.

image.altText: Hands typing on a laptop which displays a chatgpt website.

items.title: Uber Eats warns proposed e-bike laws would have 'immense' impact

children.content: Food delivery giant Uber Eats hits out at the Queensland government's push to introduce licensing mandates for e-bike and e-scooter users.

image.altText: Man with helmet and yellow jacket on e-bike with neon yellow Uber Eats bag

items.title: Remote council jobs a priority as NT Aboriginal affairs plan launched

children.content: The Northern Territory government has released its Aboriginal Affairs framework as the territory goes backwards on several Closing the Gap targets.

image.altText: Steve Edgington wears a suit and tie while standing outside NT Parliament.

items.title: Magnitude-4.5 earthquake near mine felt across large parts of NSW

children.content: The NSW Central West has been hit by the largest earthquake ever recorded in the region.

image.altText: map of nsw with blocks showing earthquake

items.title: Coalition's migration proposal faces practical questions

children.content: The Coalition's new migration policy aims to increase screening of visa applicants and "boot out" people who do not abide by Australia's values, but questions remain about how the plan would work in practice.

gallery.altText: Angus Taylor at a press conference in Parliament House.

thumbnail.altText: Angus Taylor at a press conference in Parliament House.

items.title: Gina Rinehart judgment looming in high-stakes battle over mining riches

children.content: Gina Rinehart, the wealthiest person in Australia, expects a judgment to be handed down today in an explosive court battle with the family of her father's former business partner over iron ore royalties.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart smiling and chatting to reporters at the Bush Summit in Perth.

items.title: Founder of China property giant Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud

children.content: Hui Ka Yan's plea comes after three years in detention, while Evergrande's liquidators are battling in court outside mainland China to freeze the offshore assets of the founder and his ex-spouse over billions of US dollars in dividends and remuneration.

image.altText: A close shot of a man who is China Evergrande Group Chairman Hui Ka Yan.

items.title: New lifesaving stroke prevention surgery arrives in Australia

children.content: A NSW hospital becomes the first in Australia to offer a new stroke-prevention treatment that has been available in the United States for a decade.

image.altText: Woman smiling in a whote tshirt with garden in the background

items.title: Victorian opposition pledges to toughen bail and sentencing laws

children.content: The Victorian opposition has pledged to expand the government's Adult Crime, Adult Time laws and introduce a "one-strike" bail rule that would see re-offenders automatically denied bail.

image.altText: Liberal candidate for Kew Jess Wilson speaks to media.

items.title: Dramatic dash cam vision of dangerous overtaking prompts safety call

children.content: A 21-year-old woman was charged with reckless driving after dash cam video allegedly caught her overtaking a triple road train in an "unsafe" manner.

image.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

gallery.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

thumbnail.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

items.title: The warning signs a health practitioner wants to exploit your superannuation

children.content: The healthcare regulator and tax office are cracking down on health practitioners who help patients access their superannuation early after one Perth doctor signed off on a $20,000 withdrawal for liposuction.

image.altText: A doctor and nurse in an operating room in a hospital.

items.title: Brothers living in squalor missed multiple health appointments, report finds

children.content: A 48-page report has been released on the interactions between health care workers and two siblings who were discovered living in squalor in May 2020.

image.altText: a composite image of two boys, seen from behind

items.title: Cameron Green suffers IPL nightmare with bat and ball

children.content: Cameron Green has suffered another challenging outing in the Indian Premier League as he struggles to live up to his tag as the most expensive overseas player.

image.altText: Cameron Green bowls in the IPL:

items.title: Collard handed monster ban over 'entirely unacceptable' homophobic slur

children.content: St Kilda forward Lance Collard is suspended after a marathon AFL Tribunal hearing with a deliberation of over 90 minutes.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.
